extra.message:
Path Traversal via path parameter

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into open, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.

Impact: Arbitrary file read, source code disclosure, credential theft.

Fix: Validate and sanitize the path parameter against a whitelist of allowed directories.

Path Traversal via include parameter

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into open, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.

Impact: Arbitrary file read, source code disclosure, credential theft.

Fix: Validate and sanitize the include parameter against a whitelist of allowed directories.

SQL Injection via id parameter

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into execute, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.

Impact: Data exfiltration, authentication bypass, database manipulation.

Fix: Use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation for SQL statements.

SQL Injection via comment parameter

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into execute, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.

Impact: Data exfiltration, authentication bypass, database manipulation.

Fix: Use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation for SQL statements.

Deserialization of Untrusted Data via pickle.loads()

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into pickle.loads, where it is used to deserialize an object. This may result in an Unsafe Deserialization vulnerability.

Impact: Remote code execution — an attacker can run arbitrary OS commands on the server.

Fix: Never deserialize untrusted data with pickle. Use a safe format like JSON.

Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)

Unsanitized user input from the request URL flows into re.findall, where it is used to build a regular expression. This may result in a Regular expression Denial of Service attack (ReDoS).

Impact: Denial of service through CPU exhaustion via crafted input that causes catastrophic backtracking.

Fix: Validate and sanitize user input before using it in regular expressions, or use a regex engine with backtracking limits.

Code Injection via exec() on user-controlled data

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into exec, where it is executed as Python code. This may result in a Code Injection vulnerability.

Impact: Full remote code execution — attacker can run arbitrary code on the server.

Fix: Remove this functionality entirely. Never execute dynamically loaded code from untrusted sources.

Command Injection via subprocess.run with shell=True

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into subprocess.run, where it is used as a shell command. This may result in a Command Injection vulnerability.

Impact: Full remote code execution on the server.

Fix: Pass the command as a list without shell=True: subprocess.run(["nslookup", params["domain"]], ...).

Insecure XML Parser (Python < 3.11)

xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is vulnerable to XXE and DDOS in versions of python <= 3.10. If using python runtime 3.10 or older, consider upgrading python versions, or see https://docs.python.org/3.10/library/xml.html#xml-vulnerabilities for mitigations. Python versions 3.11 and onwards do not have this issue.

Impact: XML External Entity (XXE) injection, denial of service via billion laughs attack.

Fix: Upgrade to Python 3.11+ or use defusedxml.

Insecure XML Parser (Python < 3.11)

xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is vulnerable to XXE and DDOS in versions of python <= 3.10. If using python runtime 3.10 or older, consider upgrading python versions, or see https://docs.python.org/3.10/library/xml.html#xml-vulnerabilities for mitigations. Python versions 3.11 and onwards do not have this issue.

Impact: XML External Entity (XXE) injection, denial of service via billion laughs attack.

Fix: Upgrade to Python 3.11+ or use defusedxml.

Insecure XML Parser (Python < 3.11)

xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is vulnerable to XXE and DDOS in versions of python <= 3.10. If using python runtime 3.10 or older, consider upgrading python versions, or see https://docs.python.org/3.10/library/xml.html#xml-vulnerabilities for mitigations. Python versions 3.11 and onwards do not have this issue.

Impact: XML External Entity (XXE) injection, denial of service via billion laughs attack.

Fix: Upgrade to Python 3.11+ or use defusedxml.

Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via path parameter

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into urllib.request.urlopen, where it is used as a URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server-Side Request Forgery vulnerability.

Impact: Access to internal services, port scanning, data exfiltration from internal network.

Fix: Validate the URL against a whitelist of allowed domains. Reject URLs pointing to internal/private IP ranges.

Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via include parameter

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into urllib.request.urlopen, where it is used as a URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server-Side Request Forgery vulnerability.

Impact: Access to internal services, port scanning, data exfiltration from internal network.

Fix: Validate the URL against a whitelist of allowed domains.
